articles.title: This Month at ESA: May 2026
articles.summary: What did space deliver for Europe this month? From asteroid flybys to Mars landing tests, new discoveries from Webb and Hubble, and ESA astronaut Sophie Adenot capturing shooting stars from orbit, here’s your monthly roundup from the European Space Agency.

articles.title: Space Force’s commercial gatekeeper offers a playbook for startups seeking defense business
articles.summary:
COMSO chief Col. Tim Trimailo says transparency, patience and a clear military value proposition matter as much as technical innovation
The post Space Force’s commercial gatekeeper offers a playbook for startups seeking defense business appeared first on SpaceNews.

articles.title: Bellatrix and TelePIX plan 2028 air-breathing VLEO imaging demonstration
articles.summary:
South Korean optical payload developer TelePIX and Indian propulsion specialist Bellatrix Aerospace have teamed up on a very low Earth orbit geospatial demonstration mission slated for 2028.
The post Bellatrix and TelePIX plan 2028 air-breathing VLEO imaging demonstration appeared first on SpaceNews.

articles.title: Spatial data has become a weapon of war in the US-Iran war
articles.summary:
We are in the geospatial era of warfare in which information derived from satellites is as strategically critical as territorial control. The progressive dissolution of the distinction between the civil […]

articles.title: Hubble Captures M88 on Journey to Center of Virgo Cluster
articles.summary: This NASA Hubble Space Telescope image features the active spiral galaxy Messier 88 (M88), located about 63 million light-years away.

articles.title: China’s latest batch of new and reusable rockets are close to launch
articles.summary:
A number of new Chinese state-led and commercial rockets are set for debuts and fresh first stage recovery attempts in the coming weeks and months.
